question: Will VTR's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21? Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price. Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/VTR for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used. Background: Ventas, Inc. is a leading S&P 500 real estate investment trust enabling exceptional environments that benefit a large and growing aging population. With approximately 1,400 properties in North America and the United Kingdom, Ventas occupies an essential role in the longevity economy. The Company's growth is fueled by its more than 850 senior housing communities, which provide valuable services to residents and enable them to thrive in supported environments. Ventas aims to deliver outsized performance by leveraging its operational expertise, data-driven insights from its Ventas OI platform, extensive relationships and strong financial position. The Ventas portfolio also includes outpatient medical buildings, research centers and healthcare facilities. Ventas's seasoned team of talented professionals shares a commitment to excellence, integrity and a common purpose of helping people live longer, healthier, happier lives. Ventas, Inc. was incorporated in 1983 and is based in Chicago, United States.

evidences.title: Ventas (NYSE:VTR) - Stock Analysis - Simply Wall St
evidences.content: Full year 2025 results: FFO per share: US$3.6 (up from US$3.17 in FY 2024). Revenue: US$5.82b (up 19% from FY 2024). Funds from operations (FFO): US$1.62b (up 24% from FY 2024). FFO margin: 28% (up from 27% in FY 2024). The increase in margin was driven by higher revenue. Revenue was in line with analyst estimates. Earnings per share (EPS) surpassed analyst estimates by 10.0%. Revenue is forecast to grow 13% p.a. on average during the next 3 years, compared to a 12% growth forecast for the Health Care REITs industry in the US. Over the last 3 years on average, earnings per share has increased by 85% per year but the company’s share price has only increased by 17% per year, which means it is significantly lagging earnings growth. Announcement • Feb 06 + 1 more update [...] Seeking Alpha • Nov 10 #### Ventas: Growth Set To Continue In The Years Ahead Summary Ventas is a healthcare REIT focused on senior housing, outpatient medical & research, and triple-net lease properties. The Q3 2024 results were solid and the company increased its normalized FFO midpoint outlook. With a valuation above private market alternatives and distressed peers, Ventas is tapping debt and equity markets...

evidences.title: Ventas Stock Has Surged 45% in 12 Months. Does the Valuation Still Hold Up in 2026? | TIKR.com
evidences.content: ## What Happened? Ventas, Inc. (VTR) continues to benefit from powerful demographic tailwinds in senior housing and healthcare real estate. The company reported Q1 2026 normalized FFO (Funds from Operations, the standard profitability metric for REITs, or Real Estate Investment Trusts, that adjusts for non-cash real estate depreciation) per share of $0.94, up 9% year over year. Management also raised its full-year 2026 normalized FFO guidance to a range of $3.78 to $3.88 per share, compared to the prior full-year 2025 result of $3.48. That guidance increases signals of real confidence in the underlying business. [...] Based on analysts’ consensus estimates, we used a 12.9% annual revenue growth rate. The forward 2-year consensus revenue compound annual growth rate is around 14.2%, which is robust for a healthcare REIT of this size. But revenue growth alone does not guarantee strong stock returns, because REIT valuations also depend heavily on the cost of capital and how the market prices underlying real estate assets. Ventas’s life science and medical office segments also provide meaningful diversification beyond senior housing. Its research and innovation campuses attract unive...

evidences.title: Ventas (VTR) Stock Forecast: Analyst Ratings, Predictions & Price Target 2026
evidences.content: Buy 53% Hold 27% Sell 0% Strong Sell 0% #### Bulls say Ventas possesses a well-diversified healthcare portfolio consisting of nearly 1,400 properties across various segments, including senior housing and medical office, which supports its stability and growth potential. The company's strategic presence in mature healthcare markets, including Canada and the United Kingdom, positions it advantageously in a competitive landscape, enabling it to capitalize on investment opportunities similar to those in the United States. Additionally, with approximately 1% of net operating income derived from mortgages and other loans, Ventas demonstrates a balanced approach to income generation, enhancing its financial resilience amid market fluctuations. #### Bears say [...] #### Bears say Ventas may face a negative outlook due to potential reductions in Medicare and Medicaid reimbursements, which could adversely affect rent coverage ratios and lead to necessary rent restructuring or reductions. Additionally, the company's exposure to individual tenants exceeding 5% poses risks to revenue stability, particularly if reimbursement rates decline. Furthermore, the healthcare REIT sector, characterize...

For REITs, FFO is more important than regular earnings per share because real estate depreciation can distort net income. Ventas is guiding for normal Sorry, is guiding for normalized FFO to roughly $3.82 to $3.89 per share for 2026. That gives investors a clearer picture of the company's cash flow power. Let's talk about the dividend. Ventas currently pays a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share. Annualized, that is $2.08 per share. Based on the stock Based on a stock price around the high 80s, the dividend yield is roughly in the low 2% range. So, this is not a [...] yield is roughly in the low 2% range. So, this is not a massive high-yield REIT. And if you're looking for a huge income stream today, Ventas may not be may not be the most exciting name compared to high higher-yielding REITs, BDCs, or mortgage REITs. evidences.title: Ventas: An Investment-Grade Senior And Outpatient Care REIT That Just Keeps Growing
evidences.content: Image 4: VTR - macro tailwinds VTR - macro tailwinds (Ventas investor deck) Considering that same investor deck shows this REIT investing beyond just the _US_, and in the UK and Canada, we should think about this issue in global terms. Even back in 2024, a major institution like the OECD published an article with a considerable forecast into the future: > With population ageing, the demand for helping older people with daily activities – so-called long-term care – is set to increase across OECD countries by more than one-third by 2050. So, although this need may also include at-home nursing and not just senior facilities, the role of healthcare REITs cannot be understated here, particularly ones like Ventas who also invest in outpatient care and not just senior-care. [...] ## Valuation Metrics In this section, I made the following worksheet using Seeking Alpha data and also determined a price target using the fwd P/FFO and FFO consensus: Image 13: VTR - valuation worksheet VTR - valuation worksheet (author) To unwrap this worksheet simply, Ventas is considerably overvalued both on forward price to FFO and price to book value. I include P/B as a metric since it is of particular i...

evidences.title: Ventas jumps as investors digest upbeat senior-housing update and 2026 outlook | Quiver Quantitative
evidences.content: Details:- Ventas published an investor presentation for meetings around Nareit’s REITweek 2026 conference, putting fresh emphasis on operating and portfolio trends for its senior housing operating portfolio (SHOP). - In its June 2026 business update materials, Ventas showed Q1 2026 Normalized FFO attributable to common stockholders per share of $0.94 versus $0.86 a year earlier, alongside higher Nareit FFO per share. - The same materials outlined FY 2026 guidance ranges that include Normalized FFO per share of $3.82–$3.89 and segment NOI expectations that highlight SHOP as the largest contributor. - Ventas also reaffirmed shareholder returns with a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per common share, payable July 16, 2026, to holders of record as of June 30, 2026. Sources: [...] Download our data to use for your own analysis Nancy Pelosi Profile Donald Trump Profile J. D. evidences.title: Ventas (VTR) Stock Analysis | The REIT Report
evidences.content: Welcome back to the REIT Report. Today we're looking at Ventas. I'm probably saying it wrong. I never actually heard anybody say it, but it's called Ventas Inc. And that's ticker symbol VTR. One of the biggest healthcare REITs in the market and a major player in senior housing, medical office, life science, and healthcare real estate. Ventas is not a tiny niche REIT. This is a large institutional quality real estate company with a portfolio tied to one of the strongest long-term themes in the economy, aging demographics. As the population gets older, demand for senior housing, medical care, outpatient facilities, and healthcare-related real estate should continue to grow over time. But here is the real question for investors. Is Vantas Is Ventas a great REIT to buy today? Or has the [...] expenses, labor costs, and margin expansion. That creates more upside when the business is improving, but it also creates more volatility if operating conditions get tougher. Right now, the trend is clearly positive. Ventas recently reported strong growth in its senior housing businesses with SHOP same-store cash NOI growing more than 15% year-over-year. Occupancy also improved nicely, which ma...
